name: constitutional-refinement
description: Iteratively critiques and refines LLM output against a constitution of principles. Applies each principle as a critique pass and revises until all principles are satisfied or max rounds are reached.

CONSTITUTION.description: Newline-separated list of principles to apply during critique. Each non-empty line is treated as one principle. Example: 'Be factually accurate\nAvoid harmful content\nUse clear, concise language'. All principles are evaluated in each refinement round.

API_KEY_SECRET.description: Vault path to the LLM provider API key (e.g. 'talos/openai_key' or 'talos/anthropic_key'). The key is injected by the host and never crosses into guest memory.

PROVIDER.description: LLM provider to use for critique calls. 'openai' targets api.openai.com; 'anthropic' targets api.anthropic.com.

MODEL.description: Model identifier override. Defaults to 'gpt-4o-mini' for OpenAI and 'claude-sonnet-4-6' for Anthropic. Override to use a more capable model for higher-stakes refinement (e.g. 'gpt-4o', 'claude-opus-4-6').

MAX_ROUNDS.description: Maximum number of critique-and-revise rounds. Must be between 1 and 5. The loop stops early when the model reports revision_needed: false for all remaining principles. Lower values reduce LLM cost; higher values allow more thorough refinement.

MAX_TOKENS.description: Maximum tokens the LLM may generate per critique call. Each round consumes one call, so total token usage is up to MAX_ROUNDS × MAX_TOKENS.

CRITIQUE_PROMPT.description: Optional custom instruction appended to the critique system prompt. Use to add domain-specific guidance (e.g. 'Focus on regulatory compliance language' or 'The audience is a non-technical executive'). Defaults to a generic constitutional-AI critique instruction when not set.
